user_stories.role: As a tourist planning a trip
user_stories.goal: I want to enter a destination, trip length, budget level, and interests to generate a personalised itinerary
user_stories.benefit: so I can get a ready-to-follow day-by-day plan tailored to my constraints and preferences
acceptance_criteria: The UI accepts destination, start date or trip length (days), budget level (low/medium/high) and selectable interests (e.g., museums, food, outdoors)
Submitting valid input returns a day-by-day itinerary with at least one activity per day and total days equal to trip length
Activities in the itinerary align with the selected interests and reflect the chosen budget level (e.g., fewer paid attractions for 'low' budget)
Invalid or missing required inputs return clear, user-facing validation messages

user_stories.goal: I want to view the generated itinerary on the website in a clear, navigable format
user_stories.benefit: so I can easily read, follow, and act on the plan while planning or traveling
acceptance_criteria: The website displays a day-by-day layout with each day's activities grouped and ordered
Each activity shows a title, short description, estimated time or duration, approximate cost, and a link to a map/location
The itinerary page is responsive (usable on desktop and mobile) and includes a print-friendly view
Users can expand/collapse days and copy or highlight text for external use

user_stories.role: As an external app or developer
user_stories.goal: I want a Flask API endpoint that generates itineraries from JSON input
user_stories.benefit: so other clients or services can programmatically obtain itineraries and integrate them into different interfaces
acceptance_criteria: A POST /api/itinerary endpoint accepts a JSON body with destination, trip_length, budget_level, and interests
Valid requests return HTTP 200 with a JSON response containing a day-by-day array of activities; invalid requests return HTTP 400 with error details
Responses follow a documented schema (days ordered, each day contains activities with title, description, estimated_time, and estimated_cost)
CORS and basic rate limiting are configured to allow safe cross-origin use from the website client

user_stories.role: As a traveller who wants to customise the plan
user_stories.goal: I want to tweak activities in the generated itinerary (reorder, remove, substitute) and persist my changes locally
user_stories.benefit: so I can tailor the plan to my real-world constraints without losing my adjustments
acceptance_criteria: The UI allows reordering, removing, and adding activities within each day via simple controls
Users can save their modified itinerary to browser local storage or download it, and later reopen the saved version in the web app
Saved itineraries retain the day structure and activity metadata (title, description, time, cost)
Attempting to save invalid edits (e.g., negative durations) prompts a validation error and prevents persistence

user_stories.role: As a traveller preparing for offline use
user_stories.goal: I want to export or print the itinerary so I can use it without internet access
user_stories.benefit: so I can follow the plan while travelling even when I have no connectivity
acceptance_criteria: The website offers a printable view and a downloadable PDF of the itinerary preserving day-by-day layout and activity details
The API or website provides an option to download the itinerary as structured JSON for offline tools
Exported documents include basic contact/location links and estimated costs to help on-ground decision-making
Export and print functions complete without errors for itineraries up to at least 14 days
